methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mariana Minerals is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised $85M to date. Valuation analysis relies on two primary comparables methodologies applied to a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy) supplemented by broader sector context including TAE Technologies and the public comparable Lonza (LNZA).

Stage-progression methodology, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $85M total capital raised, implies a valuation of $170M. Peer-median analysis across the 4-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $175M, with a range spanning $110M to $1.8B. The wide range reflects heterogeneity within frontier climate tech; Watershed trades at a substantial premium at $1.8B while Steady Energy anchors the lower quartile at $110M.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $144M to $201M, with a median of $172M. This narrow convergence between independent approaches supports a valuation in the $170M–$175M band. Key uncertainties center on capital efficiency relative to peers, commercialization timeline, and whether Mariana's technology positioning aligns more closely with premium outliers or the core peer median. Absent proprietary unit economics or detailed go-to-market data, the $172M median reflects fair-value positioning within observable comparables.
